title: Furniture - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Furniture 41 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Infants/Toddlers Newborns 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details GLIDE REVITALIZATION MARCH 2025 FLOOD RECOVERY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides long-term recovery assistance for Douglas County residents impacted by the March 2025 flooding. Assistance may include support with housing, home rehabilitation, debris clean up, emergency food, clothing and home furnishings, and mental health services. May be able to refer to home safety inspectors and well water testing assistance. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Residents impacted by the March 2025 flooding Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE GRESHAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 464 NE 219th Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CRIBS FOR KIDS FIND A CRIB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cribs. Crib limits are 30 pounds and 35 inches. Eligibility: Families with infants ages 0-12 months Hours: Not applicable Email (888) 721-2742 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DORCAS ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a food pantry, lunch, clothing bank, furniture, and household items. 1360 NE 9th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: -Unrestricted for most services -Food pantry: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday 9am-11am and noon-1:30pm, lunch 11am-noon Email (541) 476-2052 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER ITEMS EXCHANGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people donating furniture and appliances to those needing those items. Small items like blankets and hygiene items can be brought to the Family Resource Center. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FURNISH HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a furniture bank of new and gently used household items, including kitchen basics such as dishes, glassware, bake ware, small appliances, utensils, pot, pans, home furniture, bedding, and cleaning supplies. 1006 SE 9th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Varies by referral partner, but household must meet at least one of the following: -Individuals transitioning out of homelessness or recovery -People with disabilities or medical challenges -Refugees and people fleeing domestic violence -Foster youth and families, and aged-out foster teenagers -Veteran in the household -Families who have lost a home to a natural disaster Hours: Monday-Saturday 9am-11am (541) 638-0657 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FURNITURE SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides used furniture, kitchen, household goods, and food boxes. Accepts furniture, kitchen, and household goods donations. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK LINC MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides household goods, budgeting, job, and life skills mentoring, and connections to local food banks and community meals. May also assist with rent, electric, and propane vouchers when available. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed. Utility assistance is available once per year. 278 SE 8th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 475-4446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY GAP RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides furniture, kitchen items, firewood, medical equipment, personal hygiene supplies, cleaning supplies, and bedding/linens. Accepts donations of similar items. Assistance depends on availability. Follow-ups may be provided once the need is met. Eligibility: Low-income households (self-declared or referred and verified by caseworker, employer, or landlord) Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEWMAN UNITED METHODIST CHURCH SINGLE PARENT MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited, one time basic needs assistance. Assistance may include clothing, furniture, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots, pans, glasses, etc.), diapers and wipes, and personal hygiene supplies. One time limited assistance with utility payments such as telephone, water, electric, and gas. All payments are made directly to the vendor, and assistance is not guaranteed. The amount of assistance is on a case-by-case basis and current funding availability. 132 NE B Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Single parents with minor children Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-1pm Email (541) 479-5369 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NW FURNITURE BANK NW FURNITURE BANK VANCOUVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers gently used furniture, including dining tables, chairs, coffee and end tables, desks, nightstands, easy chairs, sofas, dressers, and bookshelves. Bed frames may be available; mattresses and box springs are not included but can be purchased at a special client rate through the retail store. Available items vary depending on inventory 13503 SE Mill Plain Boulevard #114 Vancouver, WA 98684 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (360) 787-7141 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU DOERNBECHER CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL TOM SARGENT SAFETY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dedicated to reducing preventable injuries in children by providing education, training, and a variety of childproofing equipment, safety gates, helmets, and protective gear at below-retail prices. The Tom Sargent Safety Center offers a weekday car seat inspection station by appointment at Doernbecher Children's Hospital. Certified child passenger safety technicians are available to inspect car seats and provide hands-on advice. Low-cost car seats are available for purchase for those who qualify. Can connect families to local car seat and child safety resources if they are outside of the Metro area. Portland Metro only: Can do virtual car seat inspections. The Kohl's Foundation, in partnership with DCH Tom Sargent Safety Center, is supporting a Safe Sleep Program. Through the Cribs for Kids Program, cribs and accompanying sheets are available at a reduced cost for qualifying low-income families. The goal is to help families provide a safe sleep area for their child to reduce SUID and other accidental, sleep-related infant deaths. Halo Sleep Sacks and Swaddlers are also available for purchase. 700 SW Campus Drive Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Safety education: Unrestricted For car seats and cribs, families must have a household income below 185% FPL. Children must be present at appointment or mother must be in 3rd trimester of pregnancy. Assistance depends on the zip code. 456 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN SPRINGFIELD FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
